original: Michelangelo's statue of David is unveiled in Florence
NASA launches the OSIRIS-REx probe to retrieve a rock sample from the 500-meter-wide Bennu asteroid
American inventor Richard Gurley Drew creates Scotch tape
Ruby Bridges, American civil rights activist (child who ended segregation of a Louisiana school), born in Tylertown, Mississippi
American sprinterWilma Rudolphwins her third gold medal of the Rome Olympics, anchoring the US 4 x 100 m relay team; she earlier won the 100/200 m double
Giraffe DNA study published in "Current Biology" reveals there are four species, not just one as previously assumed
Runners Jim Ryun (US) and Billy Fordjour (Ghana) collide and fall in qualifying competitions for 1500 m finals, ending Ryun's chances for gold
Kansas City A'sBert Campanerisplays all nine positions in a game
"Star Trek" premieres on NBC-TV starringWilliam ShatnerandLeonard Nimoy
American children's program "Blue's Clues" debuts on Nickelodeon cable channel
"Star Trek: The Animated Series" premieres on TV
Archaeologists uncover more than 300 gold coins from the late imperial period in a theatre in Como, Italy
Javier Sotomayor of Cuba high jumps a world record of 2.43 m
The British de Havilland DH 108 fighter flies faster than the speed of sound
Queen Elizabeth IIdies at Balmoral Castle after ruling for 70 years as the UK's longest-serving monarch; her eldest son inherits the throne as King Charles III
Abraham Lincolnsupposedly says in a speech "You may fool all the people some of the time; you can even fool some of the people all the time; but you can’t fool all of the people all the time"

Ida Henrietta Hyde, American physiologist who invented the micro-electrode and the first woman allowed to do research at Harvard Medical School and to be elected to the American Physiology Society, born in Davenport, Iowa (d. 1945)
Esther Sans Takeuchi, American scientist and engineer (invented batteries for implantable cardioverter defibrillators), born in Kansas City, Missouri[1]

Small ads in Daily Variety and Hollywood Reporter attract 437 young men interested in forming the world’s first manufactured boy band, "The Monkees." Three are chosen, with British actor-singerDavy Jonesalready cast
Star Trek premieres on television with the episode "The Man Trap"
The NASA unmanned spacecraft Genesis crash-lands when its parachute fails to open
Surveyor 5 launches and makes a soft landing on the Moon on September 10
Shuttle Discovery flies back to Kennedy Space Center via Kelly Air Force Base
Ernest Hemingway's novel "The Old Man and the Sea" is published
First permanent European settlement in the US is founded in St. Augustine, Florida
Employed byThomas Edison, William K.L. Dickson films the first boxing match in West Orange, New Jersey, an exhibition between world heavyweight championJames J. Corbettand Peter Courtney
MLB Cincinnati Reds'Pete Roseties Ty Cobb with 4,191 hits
Large statue of Confederate generalRobert E. Leeremoved from plinth on Monument Avenue, Richmond, Virginia
Europe has the warmest summer ever recorded, 0.4 degrees warmer, with August 0.8 degrees warmer, according to the Copernicus Climate Change Service
USBidenadministration releases blueprint calling for 44% of American electricity to come from the sun by 2050 (currently 3%)
First appearance of the comic strip "Blondie," created by American cartoonist Chic Young
Saundra Williams wins the first Miss Black America pageant
Inés Cuervo de Prieto, 34, gives birth to quintuplets, all boys, in Venezuela
Dutch privateerPiet Heyncaptures Spanish silver fleet in the Bay of Matanzas, Cuba
Jackie Wilsonreleases his first solo single "Reet Petite"
WINS radio station in NYC begins playing rock and roll music with Alan Freed's "Rock 'n' Roll Party" program
US 4 x 400 m relay team of Jack Yerman, Earl Young, Glenn Davis, and Otis Davis run a world record 3:02.2 to win the gold medal at the Rome Olympics
German 4 x 100 m relay team equals world record 39.5 to win the gold medal at Rome Olympics; Bernd Cullmann, Armin Hary, Walter Mahlendorf, and Martin Lauer
NFL Buffalo Bills QB Jim Kelly passes for 6 touchdowns against the Pittsburgh Steelers (52-34)

Rookie quarterback David Carr throws for 2 TDs as the Houston Texans beat the Dallas Cowboys 19-10 to become only the second expansion team, after the 1961 Minnesota Vikings, to win their inaugural game
Milwaukee Braves' future Baseball Hall of Fame pitcherWarren SpahntiesChristy Mathewsonwith 13 20-win seasons
Ken Forsch, American baseball pitcher (MLB All-Star 1976, 81; no-hitter 1979 Houston Astros; California Angels), born in Sacramento, California
Juan Manuel Fangioconfirms his F1 World Drivers' Championship, finishing 2nd in the season-ending Italian GP at Monza; takes a record 5th title by 15 points fromStirling Moss
Scottish Lotus driverJim Clarkwins the Italian Grand Prix at Monza to claim his first F1 World Drivers' Championship, Clark's fifth GP win of the season
Asha Bhosle (née Mangeshkar), Indian playback singer, Guinness World Record for most recorded vocalist, born in Sangli, British India
Buck Leonard, American Baseball HOF first baseman (Negro League World Series 1943, 44, 48 Homestead Grays; 13 x NL All Star), born in Rocky Mount, North Carolina (d. 1997)
Harry Secombe, Welsh actor, comedian, singer and goon (The Goon Show; Oliver!), born in Swansea, Wales (d. 2001)
Kenichi Horie, Japanese adventurer and solo yachtsman, born in Osaka, Japan
Cargo ship Golden Ray catches fire and capsizes in St. Simons Sound off the coast of Georgia, trapping four crew members inside
Challenger moves to the Vehicle Assembly Building at Kennedy Space Center in Florida for the mating of the STS-41G mission
NASA launches RCA-6
Two EMERCOM Il-76 aircraft land at a disaster aid staging area at Little Rock Air Force Base; it is the first time Russia has flown such a mission to North America
Ellis Island Historical Site opens on Ellis Island, New York City
John F. Kennedy Center for Performing Arts opens in Washington, D.C.
The Severn Bridge between England and Wales is officially opened byQueen Elizabeth II
Derek Jeter, Ted Simmons, Larry Walker, and Marvin Miller are inducted into the Baseball Hall of Fame in Cooperstown, New York; elected in 2020, the ceremony was postponed due to the pandemic
British TV comedy "Only Fools and Horses," created by John Sullivan and starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst, and Lennard Pearce, premieres on BBC One
Last run of the famous Pines Express over the Somerset and Dorset Railway line (UK) fittingly uses the last steam locomotive built by British Railways, 9F locomotive 92220 "Evening Star"
Turkish siege of Malta is broken by the Maltese and the Knights of St. John
Dutch surrender colony of New Netherland, including New York, to 300 English soldiers
